author: nflaig
content:
<@1019999229151821936> I opened https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5222, the branch contains tests but we can go with your testing PR for these, just added them to make sure it works as expected, the actual spec change is pretty small.

so we could either merge your PR, then I update the tests on my branch to reflect new behavior, or we merge my PR and you update the tests on your PR, ie. tests like `test_on_payload_attestation_duplicate_validator` would need to be inverted

author: potuz
content: Are you guys adding tests to check logic with repeated PTC members?

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5206 but the spec behavior is wrong imo

author: potuz
content: Yeah I think this issue is irrelevant really, whatever you add is not enforceable unless you make it a consensus change (like adding the index in the message) and then I'd be really against these changes

author: nflaig
content: I don't think you need that, there is no reason why a validator should cast 2 different votes, but their weight should be counted properly if votes are in the same slot

author: potuz
content: The forkchoice situation is really irrelevant, the only problem could be P2P downscoring but that shouldn't happen
